prd.name: Project Configuration
prd.overview: The tool bootstraps a `project.toml` for a worktree by discovering the Xcode project, listing its schemes, and recording a destination configuration that `xcodebuild` invocations downstream rely on. Configuration covers both the initial `init` flow and the runtime contract every build/test/clean command honours.

requirement.name: Project discovery
requirement.description: The tool finds the Xcode project for a worktree, either by auto-scanning the worktree root or by accepting an explicit path.
requirement.rule: When no explicit path is given, the tool scans the worktree root for `*.xcodeproj` directories and prompts the user to pick one if multiple are found.
Passing `--project <path>` to `init` selects that project directly and skips auto-discovery.
When the worktree contains an `.xcworkspace` (alone or alongside `.xcodeproj` files), the tool offers the workspace as a discoverable target on equal footing with the contained projects.

rule.context: workspace and project share a parent directory

requirement.rule: The workspace replaces the sibling project in the discovery list, matching Xcode/CocoaPods/Tuist convention that the workspace is the canonical build target.
Passing `--workspace <path>` to `init` selects that workspace directly and skips auto-discovery.

requirement.name: Build invocation
requirement.description: Every `build`, `test`, and `clean` command emits a single `xcodebuild` invocation that matches the recorded project type.
requirement.rule: When the recorded target is an `.xcodeproj`, every invocation passes `-project <path>` and the configured scheme.
When the recorded target is an `.xcworkspace`, every invocation passes `-workspace <path>` instead of `-project`, with the configured scheme resolved against the workspace.

bug.current: The tool only recognises `.xcodeproj` targets. `bootstrap.find_xcodeprojs()` scans for `*.xcodeproj` only; the `--project` flag accepts only an `.xcodeproj` path; `Project.path` is validated as a `.xcodeproj`; and every `xcodebuild` invocation hard-codes `-project`. Projects that build through an `.xcworkspace` — CocoaPods integrations, Tuist-generated workspaces, multi-project workspaces, or any setup where schemes are defined on the workspace rather than a contained project — cannot be configured. Running `init` in such a worktree either fails with "no xcodeproj found" or picks an inner `.xcodeproj` whose builds then fail because workspace-level configuration (Pods, shared schemes) is missing.
bug.expected: `init` should discover `.xcworkspace` files in addition to `.xcodeproj`, accept a `--workspace` flag, record the chosen target type in `project.toml`, and emit `-workspace` instead of `-project` for workspace targets. Scheme listing must also use `-workspace` when applicable so `xcodebuild -list` returns workspace-level schemes.
bug.steps: 1. Open a worktree whose root contains an `.xcworkspace` (e.g. a CocoaPods-integrated project where the workspace pulls in `Pods.xcodeproj` and the app project). 2. Run `worktree-ios-dev-tool init`. 3. Observe: the tool either reports "No *.xcodeproj found" (if the workspace is the only buildable target) or auto-picks an inner `.xcodeproj`. There is no way to point it at the `.xcworkspace`. 4. If an inner `.xcodeproj` is selected, subsequent `build` invocations call `xcodebuild -project <inner>.xcodeproj -scheme <name>`, which fails for workspace-only schemes or projects that depend on Pods linkage resolved at the workspace level.
